data.title: DENTSPLY SIRONA names Cueto as interim CFO
data.description:
• DENTSPLY SIRONA (NASDAQ:XRAY) said Herman Cueto has been appointed as its interim CFO, effective immediately.
  • Cueto has over two decades of experience
  • “Cueto brings deep financial acumen, operational expertise and an understanding of the global healthcare sector," CEO, Simon Campion, said.

data.title: Cerence AI CFO Tony Rodriquez sheds interim tag
data.description:
• Cerence (NASDAQ:CRNC) announced that it has appointed Antonio (Tony) Rodriquez as its permanent CFO, effective November 29, 2024.
  • Rodriquez previously served as a partner at CSuite Financial Partners, a financial executive services firm. During this time, he served as CFO of The Bouqs Company from 2019 to 2023 and as interim CFO of Cerence AI from June 2024.

data.title: Iron Mountain to offer $750M senior notes in private placement
data.description:
• Specialized REIT Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) plans to offer $750M of senior notes due 2033 in a private placement.
  • IRM shares were trading +0.01% Tuesday pre-market at $121.60.
  • The exact terms of the notes and the timing of the offering will depend upon market conditions and other factors.
  • The notes will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed by the IRM's subsidiaries that are obligors under each series of its existing notes.
  • The net proceeds are intended to be used to repay a portion of the outstanding borrowings under the company's revolving credit facility.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple to deal with class action lawsuit in UK over App Store
data.description:
• Despite trying to prevent it, Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) will now have to deal with a class action lawsuit in the U.K. from developers in the country over what they believe are anti-competitive taxes from the App Store.
  • Developers pay as much as 30% to Apple in commissions from their apps and the tech giant could wind up having to repay as much as $995M in fees to roughly 13,000 U.K. developers, Bloomberg reported. The lawsuit is being led by UK competition policy professor Sean Ennis.
  • Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Shares of the tech giant were fractionally higher in premarket trading.

data.title: Apple said to offer $1B investment in Indonesia to reverse iPhone 16 ban - report
data.description:
Indonesia has reportedly received an increased investment offer of $1 billion from Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), as the tech giant looks to reverse the ban on iPhone 16 sales in the country.

The country and the tech company have said to have settled upon the number as a “first phase” of outlay, Investment Minister Rosan Roeslani told lawmakers, according to a Bloomberg report.

Roeslani added that he expects to receive a written commitment letter from the firm in a week, the report added.

If Apple (AAPL) confirms the investment, this would make it a tenfold increase as compared to the last offer.

The minister also reaffirmed an earlier government demand that Apple should offer Indonesia a better deal than Vietnam, the Bloomberg report said.

The iPhone-marker had previously proposed to invest around $100M over two years in Indonesia. Prior to that, the firm had offered to invest around $10M in Indonesia, which included investment in a factory in Bandung that makes accessories and components.

Last month, the Indonesian Industry Minister Agus Gumiwang Kartasasmita had said that amount (($100M)) was inadequate compared to what Apple has invested in other countries.

In October, Indonesia had restricted Apple (AAPL) from selling its new iPhone 16 series in the country, noting that the company has yet to meet local investment requirements.

Indonesia's assertive efforts to boost domestic manufacturing has worked in the past. ByteDance's (BDNCE) TikTok invested $1.5B in a joint venture with local marketplace Tokopedia, a move that helped it resume services after a ban on social media e-commerce.

data.title: Upstart upgraded to Buy at Redburn on positive inflection
data.description:
Redburn Atlantic upgraded Upstart Holdings (NASDAQ:UPST) to Buy from Neutral after the lending platform used by banks and credit unions delivered results that exceeded expectations for two straight quarters and forward guidance provided another incremental positive.

"Following 3Q24, we observe a clear positive inflection in fundamentals, driven almost entirely by the company’s latest AI innovations, which still leaves upside potential from tailwinds related to falling US interest rates," analyst Simon Clinch wrote in a note to clients.

Upstart's (NASDAQ:UPST) combination of AI credit technology and its technology platform business model should allow "meaningful market share gains over time, as well as high incremental margins and significant scalability," he added.

That could drive the share price to over $250 over the next five years, Clinch figured.

Redburn Atlantic increased its price target for Upstart (UPST) to $95 from $37.

Upstart (UPST) stock rose 2.0% in premarket trading to $68.76 at 8:21 AM ET.

Clinch's Buy rating on the stock contrasts with the SA Quant rating, average SA Analyst rating, and average Wall Street rating, all at Hold.

data.title: Jacobs selected for $3.3B renewable energy project in the Philippines
data.description:
Jacobs (NYSE:J) said Tuesday that SP New Energy Corp. selected it to deliver engineering services, technical advice and construction phase services, for a 3,500 MW solar panel and 4,500 MW battery energy storage system in the Philippines.

The $3.3B Terra Solar project is expected to become one of the world's largest solar farms and supply 12% of the Philippines' total energy demand when complete.

Jacobs (J) said it will use advanced digital construction management tools and drone technology to manage field tasks, streamline site inspections and track progress across the 11.5 square mile site north of Manila.

"The Terra Solar project will significantly reduce the country's reliance on coal, decrease energy-related emissions and bolster progress toward a more sustainable, secure and resilient energy system," Jacobs (J) VP Fiachra Ó Cléirigh said.

data.title: Humana names new CFO, reiterates annual profit forecast
data.description:
• Humana (NYSE:HUM) has announced that Celeste Mellet will be appointed to succeed Susan Diamond as chief financial officer, effective Jan 11, 2025.
  • Diamond is stepping down after an 18-year career at the managed care insurer, including over three years as CFO, and will serve in an advisory role through the end of 2025 to ensure a smooth transition.
  • Mellet currently serves as CFO of infrastructure fund manager Global Infrastructure Partners, which was recently acquired by BlackRock.
  • Separately, Humana (HUM) reiterated its 2024 adjusted earnings per share guidance of ‘at least $16.00’ (consensus estimate: $16.14) and its 2025 adjusted EPS commentary of ‘at least in line with final 2024 results.’

data.title: Endeavor Group Holdings declares $0.06 dividend
data.description:
• Endeavor Group Holdings (NYSE:EDR) declares $0.06/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.79%
  • Payable Dec. 30; for shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • See EDR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: United Community Banks to acquire ANB Holdings in an all-stock deal
data.description:
• Financial holding company United Community Banks (NYSE:UCB) is set to acquire ANB Holdings, and the unit American National Bank, in an all-stock transaction.
  • UCB shares were trading -1.04% Tuesday pre-market at $33.42.
  • The transaction is valued at ~$80M.
  • The two companies have executed a definitive merger agreement.
  • ANB shareholders will receive 1.650 shares of United Community Banks for each share of ANB.
  • The merger is expected to be accretive to UCB's earnings per share by ~$0.04 per share in 2026, the first full year of combined operations.
  • The merger agreement was approved by the boards of both the companies.
  • The merger is expected to close in Q2 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
